title: Pancakes – foolproof
excerpt: With the cold now closing in on us, the craving for pancakes is closing in on me too. Last night was just such a night and I whipped up the tried-and-trusted recipe. The secret of pancakes is that you have to let the batter rest for at least an hour before …
html:
With the cold now closing in on us, the craving for pancakes is closing in on me too. Last night was just such a night and I whipped up the tried-and-trusted recipe. The secret of pancakes is that you have to let the batter rest for at least an hour before you cook it.

The batter makes about 10 thin pancakes.

YOU'LL NEED:

1 cup cake flour
¼ teaspoon salt
½ teaspoon baking powder
2 eggs
2 cups water (or 1 cup milk and one cup water)
1 tablespoon oil

 

NOW:

  1. sift the flour, baking powder and salt together
  2. whisk the eggs and water/milk together in a cup
  3. now whisk the liquid into your flour mixture – whisk until smooth
  4. let it rest for 1 hour
  5. heat your pan on the stove with a small bit of oil
  6. pour in enough batter to just cover the bottom of the pan
  7. when the batter looks dry, turn the pancake with a spatula
  8. cook your pancakes – make nice thin pancakes
  9. stack on a plate and sprinkle cinnamon sugar over

What you put inside the pancakes is endless. Just as good with a savoury filling, melted cheese, mayo chicken or tuna.

Sweet fillings like caramel, chocolate and apple make the pancakes a winner.

I like to eat mine with cinnamon sugar and a little bit of lemon juice.
